title: Best Editing Nominees at the 2026 Oscars, Ranked
text: Collider ranked all Best Editing nominees: 5) Marty Supreme - Ronald Bronstein and Josh Safdie bring frenetic energy to the table tennis drama with momentum throughout. Competition sequences are crafted like a full-blown sports drama, immersing viewers in the protagonist's experience. 4) Sinners - Michael P. Shawver demonstrates impressive timing with the vampire film, knowing how to escalate excitement. His work includes a memorable single-take sequence and strategic reorganization of narrative structure. He cleverly intercuts flashback moments during emotional peaks. 3) Sentimental Value - Olivier Bugge Coutté handles an ambitious multi-generational family saga spanning a century, jumping back and forth through time liberally with perspective shifts and meta-fictional elements. His editing emphasizes subtle character moments that deepen audience understanding. 2) F1 - Stephen Mirrione, an Oscar winner previously for Traffic, crafts an intense racing film spanning the globe, helping viewers feel every twist and turn with multiple racing perspectives positioned simultaneously. 1) One Battle After Another - Andy Jurgensen, in only his second feature editing role, creates seamless storytelling. The narrative glides flawlessly across action sequences and character moments with minimal viewer awareness of editing craft.
